title: Stable transfection of unicellular relative of animals,  Corallochytrium limacisporum, using Lonza Nucleofector
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6g039","text":"This protocol describes the transfection of Corallochytrium limacisporum, one of the unicellular relatives of animals. The transfection is performed using Lonza Nucleofector System X and reporter cassettes that contain endogenous regulatory elements of C. limacisporum. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":44,"length":28},{"style":"italic","offset":253,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4fnhd","text":"Keeps cells on ice during the whole procedure \u0026 all reagents must be ice-cold","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"80gbv","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6b1o6","text":"Main materials:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"csqqf","text":"P3 PrimaryCell 4D-Nucleofector Kit S (32 rxn) Kit Catalog# H3V4XP-5032","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"97mgg","text":"pUC19 carrier plasmid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6d3gj","text":"highly concentrated reporter construct ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"evfc4","text":"1XPBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7qncl","text":"Marine Broth","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4b438","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4qls9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inoculate 100 μl of C. limacisporum culture in 5ml of Marine Broth in 25cm 2 flask. Keep at 23°C air incubator for two days.
Scrape gently and count cells to reach 1.5E6 cells / condition using hemocytometer If there are more conditions, it is recommended to pull the cells together to get more visible pellet
Spin down the cells at 1500xg for 5' Discard the supernatant.
Wash the pellet with an ice-cold 1XPBS (100 μl / condition) Spin down and discard the supernatant.
Transfer the mix (cells + DNA plasmid) into a strip (from the manufacture kit) Do it carefully & without creating bubbles Final volume: 20-25 μl
Insert into Lonza nucelofection system and apply code: EN - 138
After transfection, immediately add 80 μl of Marine Broth medium (MB) to a well Mix up and down
Check for positive cells 24 - 48h post-transfection using fluorescent microscopy
Replace 500 μl of medium with fresh MB that contains 300 μg/ml of puromycin Increase of mCherry-positive cells can be observed over time.
Add DNA mix to the cell pellet

Mix DNA plasmids: 10 μg of an reporter plasmid + 20 μg carrier DNA (pUC19) It is recommended that plasmid is highly concentrated (2-5 μg/ul) to avoid final buffer dilution with the DNA solution Our plasmid contains mCherry coding sequence & resistance to puromycin gene ( pac ) under constitutive promoter: actin (Addgene: #104446) or tubulin (Addgene#129560) Note: 10 μg of reporter plasmids guarantees the highest efficiency, however a successful transfection can be also obtained with 5 and 1μg of the plasmid
Transfer DNA mix to 20 μl of ice-cold P3 buffer It is recommended not to keep the cells in P3 buffer for too long
Transfer to 12-well plate (NUNC) that contains 1 ml of growth medium (MB) and incubate overnight
creator: Aleksandra Kozyczkowska
